name: controller
summary: REST endpoint handler that maps an HTTP method and path to a single controller class.
description: Creates a REST controller and registers it in an initializer
problem: You want to expose an action over HTTP — a single endpoint that takes a request, does work, and returns a response — without sprawling routing files or framework-specific glue.
appliesWhen: You need to expose a single piece of functionality over HTTP
The action maps cleanly to one HTTP method on one path
You want the route, validation, and middleware to live with the handler
Consumers are external (frontend, mobile, third party) or internal API clients
avoidWhen: The action is internal-only and called by other PHP code (use a service class)
You need a GraphQL operation (use phpnomad/graphql-type)
You need a CLI entry point (use phpnomad/command)
You're building a long-running task triggered by a job queue (use phpnomad/task)
examples: Add a POST endpoint to create an order
Expose a DELETE endpoint for removing a subscription
Add an API route to list subscriptions for a user
tradeoffs: One controller per endpoint by design — discoverable and testable, but produces more files than route-list-style frameworks. Capabilities (middleware, validation, interceptors) plug in via traits, not config.
outputs: A {name} controller class with a handle() method
A registration adding the controller to the initializer's getControllers() list
postApply: Implement handle() with the actual request processing
Add middleware, validations, or interceptors via the WithMiddleware/WithValidations/WithInterceptors traits
If the path needs the runtime API base, use the WithEndpointBase trait
Verify the route shows up via `phpnomad inspect:routes`

name.aiHint: Verb-noun PascalCase describing the action (GetUsers, CreateOrder, DeleteSubscription). One controller per action.

method.description: HTTP method (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH)
method.aiHint: Uppercase HTTP verb. GET for reads, POST for creates, PUT/PATCH for updates, DELETE for removals.

endpoint.aiHint: Path-only, leading slash. Use {param} placeholders for path variables (e.g. /users/{id}). Don't include the API base — use WithEndpointBase trait if needed.

initializer.description: FQCN of the initializer to register in
initializer.aiHint: Pick the initializer that owns related controllers for this domain. If unsure, use the project's root initializer.
